entries.name: BadRequest
entries.description: Returned by async-graphql-axum's GraphQLRequest extractor when the request body cannot be parsed into a GraphQL request. Triggers: malformed JSON body (InvalidRequest), JSON-array batch sent to the single-request endpoint (UnsupportedBatch), body-stream I/O failure (Io), or — if Content-Type is multipart/form-data — multipart parse failures (InvalidMultipart, MissingOperatorsPart, MissingMapPart, MissingFiles, NotUpload, InvalidFilesMap). Response body is the Rust Debug repr of the underlying ParseRequestError variant. The indexer's transform_lentgh_limit_exceeded middleware rewrites only the body-limit-overflow case to 413; all other 400s pass through unchanged. Note: GraphQL semantic/validation errors do NOT produce 400 — they return HTTP 200 with errors[] inside the response body.
fixes: Inspect the response body — it identifies the ParseRequestError variant in Debug format (e.g. 'InvalidRequest(...)', 'UnsupportedBatch')
Send well-formed JSON shaped as { "query": "...", "variables": {...}, "operationName": "..." }
Use Content-Type: application/json — the indexer does not expose multipart upload routes
Send one query per request; the single-request endpoint does not accept JSON-array batches
